Ideally located in the upscale embassy district, Fiesta Residences has a quiet and peaceful ambiance, yet is just five minutes from the Osu neighborhood, home to the city’s most vibrant bars, cafés, and shops. - Choose from 24 guestrooms and suites, or 30 apartments with kitchens that have washers and dryers - Enjoy the convenience of daily housekeeping and room service - Take advantage of complimentary airport transfers - Dine on succulent steak and seafood at The Grillroom - Work out at the gym with treadmills, exercise bikes, and weight machines - Relax in the Lurra Spa, with African-inspired treatments and bespoke facials Combining the privacy of your own apartment with the service and facilities of a five-star hotel, Fiesta Residences is the top choice for extended stays in Accra. The welcoming staff can arrange all aspects of your stay, from exchanging foreign currency to organizing a driver to take you to business meetings or leisure outings.

SHAPR.name: Primus Residence Shanghai Hongqiao
SHAPR.address_raw: No 188 Lane 1588 ZhuGuang Road, Qingpu Qu
SHAPR.description: Located in the leafy, affluent suburb of Hongqiao, Primus Residence Shanghai Hongqiao is a gorgeous address of distinction, offering easy access to transport hubs. With a dashing collection of private apartment suites, modern in design and featuring separate living spaces with full kitchens, Primus Residence Shanghai Hongqiao sports an ensemble of amenities that brings a home-away-from-home feel to the exciting metropolis. From a stylish library-themed bar to accommodating service at any hour of the day, this hotel is your urban retreat for any stay in Shanghai. - Decide what best suits your stay by choosing between 158 fully furnished studios and suites - Come home to clean long-term accommodations with regular housekeeping - Make your way about town with ease, given close proximity to the airport as well as metro and railway stations - Stroll to the National Exhibition and Convention Center (Shanghai), only a 10-minute walk away - Enjoy a pairing of tapas and your favorite cocktail with a fireside book at The Library There are plenty of complimentary perks that come with a stay at Primus Residence Shanghai Hongqiao, as the staff’s main mission is to make your visit to Shanghai breezy and seamless. For instance, there is a complimentary shuttle that travels between the hotel and the airport, and if you are driving, parking is included. For work-related trips, make use of the hotel’s two meeting rooms, conference center, and public area WiFi.

MIAEH.name: The Elser Hotel and Residences Miami
MIAEH.description: The Elser Hotel Miami – an All-Suite Hotel – is located in the epicenter of Miami’s booming Downtown neighborhood, close to all the excitement of the Magic City. The Elser Hotel offers Studio Suites, 1-Bedroom, 2-Bedroom and 3-Bedroom Suites, each with full en-suite bathrooms per bed, providing unique comfort and privacy, and abundant living space. Guests have access to spectacular amenities, including a multi-level modern fitness complex with sprawling views and a dedicated fitness studio for daily fitness classes, a large rooftop pool with kid-friendly beach entry, a 16 person sky view hot-tub, a rooftop event lawn, and full cocktail bar overlooking Biscayne Bay and the Miami skyline. Highlights of the resort include: - 450 residential-style guest accommodations with floor-to-ceiling windows and walk-out balconies, full kitchens with luxury condo appliances, California style closets, and full-size washers and dryers - A luxurious pool with creative cocktails overlooking the waters of Biscayne Bay - Small plates like salads, oysters, and artisan pizzas served at Vice Versa - Outdoor yoga classes plus a two-story, state-of-the-art fitness center Guests can also take advantage of complimentary exclusive beach club access on South Beach, utilize our in-house physical therapy partners Pinecrest Physical Therapy, or reserve a private yacht experience. The hotel’s location in the nucleus of Miami allows for easy access to the Port of Miami, Miami Beach, Bayside Marketplace, Brickell, the Kaseya Center, Wynwood, Midtown, Coral Gables, Coconut Grove, and the Design District.

MAHAM.name: Amagatay Menorca
MAHAM.address_raw: Finca Torralba Gran, Carretera Alaior a Calan Porter S/N, Alaior Menorca
MAHAM.description: Authentic Menorcan architecture and rustic luxury come together at Amagatay Menorca, a 19th-century farm where olive green and toasted brown color palettes draw the natural world in. Despite its relaxed atmosphere, the hotel gives off an air of modern sophistication, with accommodations influenced by the Mediterranean with ample natural light, open spaces, minimalist furnishings, and areas where guests can view or connect with the local landscape. Enjoy a romantic al fresco meal at Amagatay Restaurant, pamper yourself with open-air massage treatments, flow through a private yoga class, explore the countryside by bicycle or horseback, and toast to the day as the sun disappears at the Sunset Bar. Highlights of the hotel include: - 20 understated guestrooms and suites, many with a lush garden or sprawling island views - A romantic Mediterranean restaurant and a farm-to-table grill, both with outdoor terraces - A Sunset Bar with a special menu and fine wines to sip as the day winds down - Rejuvenating in-room or outdoor massage treatments that cultivate relaxation and inner peace - Active adventures, from yoga sessions and swimming to biking, boating, and horseback riding

MADHA.name: ARTIEM Madrid
MADHA.description: For travelers to Spain’s capital seeking an urban escape close to the city’s best sights and attractions—but world’s away when it comes to interior atmosphere—ARTIEM Madrid is the perfect Iberian home base. Located in the heart of Madrid, the hotel is comprised of 83 understated guestrooms, an expansive fitness center, and The Green, a warm, welcoming space open 24 hours a day offering breakfast, complimentary grab-and-go snacks, and an “honesty bar” where guests can create their own cocktails. Guests of ARTIEM Madrid can also take advantage of its enviable location just outside the city center. Highlights of the hotel include: - 83 sleek, unadorned guestrooms creating calm, quiet settings for a relaxing stay - An all-day eatery with an emphasis on breakfast that transitions into a snack bar and study area - Fitness center with state-of-the-art equipment and an emphasis on personal training - Chill Out Terrace, where guests can unwind with friends, family, or business associates - An urban oasis of greenery meant to encourage rest and relaxation

LASDG.name: Downtown Grand Hotel & Casino
LASDG.description: For travelers to Las Vegas, Nevada, The Strip and its neon lights, over-the-top casinos, and endless entertainment are in the undeniable hub of the city. But for those seeking a more authentic experience—one where the city’s culture, food, and local entertainers take center stage—Downtown Grand Hotel & Casino is the perfect off-Strip place to stay in Sin City. Its out-of-the-way locale allows the hotel to flourish in ways others cannot, with amenity-rich stays designed for families, friends, couples, multigenerational groups, and even solo business travelers. Set in the heart of downtown, guests will enjoy easy access to nearby attractions such as The Mob Museum and the Fremont Street Experience, while the Strip and its casinos are just a short drive away. Highlights of the hotel include: - An impressive 1,124 guestrooms and suites with a stunningly chic design and desert views - Delicious, dry-aged steaks from award-winning Triple George Grill Steakhouse - A poolside paradise with shaded cabanas, daybeds, and lawn games overlooking the skyline - Classic American tastes and tunes with live music at the Freedom Beat restaurant - A lively casino offering Roulette, Blackjack, slot machines, and more - A vast art collection, featuring paintings, photographs, and immersive installations

FSZFM.name: Fujisan Mishima Tokyu Hotel
FSZFM.description: Guests are welcomed into a soaring lobby, accented with shoji screens and locally quarried lava rock that provides travelers with an immediate sense of place. From there, a multilingual staff dedicated to personalized service will check you in and see you to your room in Japan's Kamo District, just two hours south of Tokyo. Whether traveling for business on a two-day trip to meet with local executives or seeking culture and solitude as a family, Fujisan Mishima Tokyu Hotel welcomes guests to an eco-adjacent retreat between the mountains and the sea. Highlights of the hotel include: - 195 well-appointed guestrooms with quintessential Japanese touches - Traditional Japanese robatayaki cuisine with stunning views of Mount Fuji - Relaxing baths on the hotel’s 14th-floor indoor-and-outdoor onsen, plus sauna and steam room - Bicycles for guests to use to explore nearby attractions - Close to Fuji-Hakone National Park plus Tokyo day trips via a 45-minute bullet train ride

TYOFT.name: Futakotamagawa Excel Hotel Tokyu
TYOFT.description: Nestled minutes away from the bustle and nightlife of Shibuya, Futakotamagawa Excel Hotel Tokyu is a riverside escape in the Futakotamagawa District of the upscale suburb of Setagaya-ku. Known as a shopper’s paradise, this high-rise hotel places you steps from the alluring stores, cinemas, and gardens of the Futako-Tamagawa Rise, while providing effortless access to downtown Tokyo’s signature stops via train trips out of the nearby Futako-Tamagawa Station. Take in Tama River views straight from your room, unwind in the public bath, and soar above the city at The 30th Restaurant during your Japanese journey to Futakotamagawa Excel Hotel Tokyu. - Choose from Tama River or city views from your room. On a clear day, you may even spot Mt Fuji in the distance - Head to the tower’s 29th floor to relax and recover in the hotel’s complimentary public bath - Enjoy dining with a view as you overlook the city from floor-to-ceiling windows at The 30th Restaurant - Hop aboard a train at Futako-Tamagawa Station for a 10-minute trip to the buzzy Shibuya Station - Take a stroll to the specialty shops, department stores, restaurants, and gardens at Futako-Tamagawa Rise

VERGP.name: Galeria Plaza Veracruz
VERGP.address_raw: Boulevard Adolfo Ruiz Cortines 3495, Veracruz Boca del Rio
VERGP.description: Modern luxury meets coastal tranquility at Galeria Plaza Veracruz. With the warm sands of the Gulf Coast, designer shops, and ecotourism moments away, you’re invited to unwind and recharge. Visit the Plaza Las Americas shopping mall — directly connected to the hotel via the lobby — then dip into the glass-roofed pool that is surrounded by luscious greenery. Watch the ocean from your balcony, as each of the guestrooms has one. Go directly from shopping or a business meeting to lounging by the pool or hop on the free shuttle to the hotel’s own beach club. With direct access to the World Trade Center, you do not have to leave the hotel for events and conventions. Highlights of the hotel include: - 258 contemporary guestrooms and suites designed for guests to rest and recharge - Gourmet Mexican cuisine at the hotel’s signature restaurant, The Mulatto - Amenities, such as an indoor pool, hot tub, business center, and kid’s club - Fitness center with top-of-the-line equipment as well as yoga and spin classes - Dedicated, off-site beach club where guests can unwind on the sand with a cocktail in hand

FUKHE.name: Hakata Excel Hotel Tokyu
FUKHE.description: Resting seaside between the underground shopping boutiques and famed ramen shops of Japan’s Fukuoka Prefecture, Hakata Excel Hotel Tokyu puts you in the hub of the Hakata Ward. Chic and spacious suites with beckoning bayviews will tempt you to cross the Naka River where you’ll find yourself in the heart of downtown Tenjin. Stop for a meditative moment in Kego Park, grab a bite at yatai (food stalls), or head up ACROS Fukuoka for a spectacular view of the city. Whether shopping your way through Fukuoka or stopping by on business, Hakata Excel Hotel Tokyu offers an unforgettable experience. At A Glance: - Enjoy traditional Japanese hotpot at Naginoki or head to Keyaki for grill-house style dining—both on property - Have to catch a flight? Nakasu-Kawabata Station is within walking distance and runs straight to the airport - Take a stroll to the entertainment center of Nakasu where riverside streets are lined with bars and restaurants - Treat yourself at the nearby “Ramen Stadium,” explore Kushida Shrine, and relax by the water garden at Ōhori Park - Three on-site meeting rooms ranging from large banquet halls to small conference spaces

MMJHT.name: Hakuba Tokyu Hotel
MMJHT.description: Neighboring the towering Japanese Northern Alps and home to one of Japan’s most-famous onsens, Hakuba Tokyu Hotel is a year-round destination attracting skiers, snowboarders, and spa-lovers for the perfect combination of adventure and relaxation. The lodge-style ski resort features 102 rooms and suites, and boasts everything from dazzling mountain views and transportation to the slopes to first-class French cuisine and authentic Shabu-Shabu. Whether exploring the Alps or enjoying spa-and-springs pampering, head to Hakuba Tokyu Hotel for a majestic mountainside escape. At A Glance - Take in awe-inducing views of the soaring Alps from your room’s private balcony - Three on-site dining options serve everything from French-style cuisine to authentic Japanese dishes - Relax and recover at the Grand Spa’s Happo Hot Spring famous for its high alkalinity and healing properties - Explore the Alps via skis, snowboards, or snowshoes. There is an on-site rental shop and lessons are available - Shuttle to the slopes, relax with aromatherapy at the spa, and dip into the indoor-outdoor hot springs

TYOHE.name: Haneda Excel Hotel Tokyu
TYOHE.description: Resting on the 7th floor of Haneda Airport’s Terminal 2, Haneda Excel Hotel Tokyu is a convenient retreat where imported Scandinavian wood meets panoramic soundproof glass to form more than 300 guestrooms. Peaceful views of planes passing through and the Tokyo Bay Area are accompanied by meeting spaces, a business center, and all-day dining at the aptly named Flyer’s Table. Whether you’re waiting for your next flight, heading to Haneda for a meeting, or craving a heaping dose of Japanese culture at Tokyo’s top districts, Haneda Excel Hotel Tokyu boasts a first-class stay. - Prefer peaceful rest or runway views? The hotel’s soundproof rooms and suites manage to blend both - Spot planes whizzing through the air as you dine on Japanese or Western dishes at Flyer’s Table - Take a trip to explore the skyscrapers at Shinjuku or the lively Shibuya District via train, bus, or taxi - Cozy meeting spaces and rooms equipped with projectors, WiFi, and an attentive staff

STOCS.name: Hotel C Stockholm
STOCS.description: Whether it's your first visit to Stockholm or your fifth, Hotel C Stockholm is dedicated to ensuring your stay is imbued with Swedish style, comfort, and culture. Set in the heart of the city, the hotel serves as the perfect launch pad for exploring the cobbled streets, timber-framed neighborhoods, and other attractions spread across Stockholm’s 14 unique islands. If you’re new to the city or need a refresher on top attractions, how to get around, or where to eat and drink, stop by the hotel’s Stockholm Info Desk, where experienced specialists can help you plan your itinerary, make reservations, or offer suggestions tailored to you. Highlights of the hotel include: - 367 cozy guestrooms with mural walls featuring large-scale photos of Stockholm’s sights - Traditional Scandinavian fare served at the hotel’s restaurant, Swede Hollow - A gourmet buffet breakfast of savory and sweet treats to start your morning off right - Experience sub-zero temperatures and ice-cold drinks at nightlife hotspot ICEBAR

FSZIZ.name: Izu-Imaihama Tokyu Hotel
FSZIZ.description: Lined by the Pacific’s azure waters and known for its dreamy white sand beaches, Japan’s Izu Peninsula is home to Izu-Imaihama Tokyu Hotel. Surrounded by famed hot springs and lush natural beauty — the nearby hiking trails are one of a kind – this scenic seaside resort is the perfect getaway from the urban din of Tokyo. Whether spotting the Izu Shichito Islands from your room, exploring Kawazu Nanadaru’s seven waterfalls, or unwinding in the nearby hot springs, this picture-perfect oceanfront oasis provides easy access to the best of the region. Highlights of the hotel include: - 134 guestrooms and suites, many of which offer ocean views from the balcony - Varied dining, ranging from Japanese haute cuisine to mouthwatering teppanyaki - Eclectic ways to unwind, including aromatherapy spa treatments and hot spring baths - Exciting amenities, such as an outdoor pool, a game room for children, and a library

KMQKT.name: Kanazawa Tokyu Hotel
KMQKT.description: Steps from one of the Three Great Gardens of Japan, notable modern art works, and Omicho Market’s extraordinary seafood, an elegant escape in Kanazawa’s city center awaits at Kanazawa Tokyu Hotel. The ancient castle city is a symbol of traditional Japanese culture, and this well-connected hotel is the ideal home base to experience everything from the cherry blossoms and famed fountains at Kenrokuen Garden to Leandro Erlich's ‘Swimming Pool’ at the 21st Century Museum of Contemporary Art. Whether pairing Kaga cuisine with mountain views at Kincharyou or staying onsite for an assortment of events, Kanazawa Tokyu Hotel is a genuine Japanese jewel. At A Glance - Stroll through the ancient trees and ponds at Kenrokuen Garden and stop by Omicho Market for famed fresh fish - Walk to the 21st Century Museum of Contemporary Art to see Turrell’s ‘Blue Planet Sky’ and Erlich’s ‘Swimming Pool’ - Savor Western and Japanese dishes at Marais D’or or head to Kincharyou for Kaga cuisine and sweeping views - On-site conference rooms and banquet halls host everything from cozy groups of 40 to large groups of 800 - Exploring the area is easy with the Korinbo bus stop right outside and the Kanazawa JR Station a short drive away

SHATL.name: Longemont Shanghai
SHATL.description: Like the city it calls home, Longemont Shanghai is an ultra-modern destination, offering luxury, comfort, and sanctuary from the buzz of Shanghai below. Whether you are treating yourself to a foot massage, admiring an impressive view of the city skyline from an indoor rooftop sun lounger, or savoring the delightful tastes of Italy in one of China’s busiest cities, this five-star hotel delivers it all in elegant surroundings with distinguished grace. Highlights of the hotel include: - 510 guestrooms and suites offer stunning city views through floor-to-ceiling windows - Restaurants offering varied cuisine, from Asian fusion and Cantonese to Italian - Exquisite J Spa with relaxing treatments featuring exotic Chinese flair - Panoramic heated swimming pool with commanding views of Shanghai from above - A state-of-the-art fitness center with modern equipment, from stair climbers to a wave machine - Prestigious club lounge with complimentary breakfast and evening cocktail hour

IZOME.name: Matsue Excel Hotel Tokyu
IZOME.description: Scenic waterways flow by ancient castles and samurai streets in Japan’s historic “City of Water”, Matsue. From sunset cruises on the Horikawa River and coastal hot springs to a 17th-century castle and sumptuous seafood, the traditional town in the Shimane Prefecture is a hotbed for Japanese culture and serene surroundings. Nestled in the city center just steps from JR Matsue Station, Matsue Excel Hotel Tokyu gives you easy access to the Chūgoku region and a modern room to call home in the idyllic Japanese city on Honshu Island. At a Glance - Well connected in downtown Matsue, you’ll be steps from JR Matsue Station and bus stops - Head across the street to the tourist center to round out your trip’s itinerary with local hot spots and sightseeing - Dine on your favorite Western and Japanese dishes at the lobby-level Shangri La restaurant - Hosting an event? Banquet facilities, conference rooms, and on-site parking available - Admire Matsue Castle’s black walls, hit Lake Shinji for sunset, and check out the paintings at Adachi Museum of Art

MMYMT.name: Miyakojima Tokyu Hotel & Resorts
MMYMT.description: Sitting seaside on the island paradise of Okinawa, Miyakojima Tokyu Hotel & Resorts is a top spot for a tropical isle escape in Japan. After a short stroll through the surrounding gardens, pop off your flip flops to hit the white sand and azure waters of Yonaha Maehama Beach or lay back on a lounger by the hotel’s sprawling blue-tile pools. Bright rooms match the tropical vibes of the island as you admire the sunset from a private balcony after exotic pampering at Spa Eurlia. Striking the perfect balance between spacious accommodations, spectacular scenery, and Japanese cuisine, Miyakojima Tokyu Hotel & Resorts is the ideal Okinawa escape. Highlights of the hotel include: - 246 guestrooms mere steps from the beach, most with a balcony and ocean views - Three Japanese restaurants, from buffets to signature sushi, plus a piano lounge - A spa offering blissful massage treatments and exfoliating facials with natural ingredients - Multiple beachfront swimming pools with lounge chairs and umbrellas - Adventurous activities ranging from snorkeling and sea turtle viewing to parasailing

NGOYA.name: Nagoya Tokyu Hotel
NGOYA.description: Nagoya Tokyu Hotel offers a distinct fusion of Japanese perfection and European elegance in the downtown district. This serene hideaway offers modern facilities and amenities, welcoming guests with a soaring lobby framed by grand pillars, spacious and serene guestrooms, and restaurants serving meals ranging from authentic local Japanese dishes to fine French cuisine. From its vast facilities to meticulous service from an ever-gracious staff, the hotel is an ideal base for exploring the temples, castle, towers, and shopping malls of Nagoya. Highlights of the hotel include: - 564 understated, European-style guestrooms and suites with luxury services and amenities - Varied dining venues, encompassing French and Chinese cuisines in addition to Japanese - A state-of-the-art fitness club with gym equipment, massages, sauna, and heated pool - Tokyu Ave shopping zone with more than 10 shops offering clothing, jewelry, and more - Wedding and banquet halls, a photo studio, a beauty salon, an atrium lounge, and more

IBZOD.name: OCEAN DRIVE Ibiza
IBZOD.description: Embrace your passion for music at this boutique hotel, where DJs and records are celebrated at every turn. Located on Ibiza, one of Spain’s Balearic Islands, Ocean Drive Ibiza is steps away from the Mediterranean Sea and minutes away from old town Dalt Vila, a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Whether you’re heading out to Ibiza’s famous late-night clubs or staying at the hotel to soak up the sun, Ocean Drive Ibiza has you covered. Stay in suites inspired by prominent musicians like Armin Van Buuren and Joseph Capriati, retreat to the vinyl corner in the lobby to listen to records, or visit the rooftop bar to see DJs spin live. Highlights of the hotel include: - 40 guestrooms and suites with sprawling views of the mountains, ocean, or Ibiza’s old town - A restaurant with all-day dining, including a breakfast buffet that runs until noon - Rooftop oasis with sea breezes, live DJ music, and a sky bar serving signature cocktails - A vinyl corner honoring music with a record player, a collection of albums, and space to lounge - Concierge service to assist with water sport reservations, club recommendations, and more

OSAOE.name: Osaka Excel Hotel Tokyu
OSAOE.description: Part of the prestigious Tokyu Hotels, this central Osaka escape is an ideal place to stay when visiting Japan's second-largest city. Set along the famous Midosuji Avenue, lined by ginkgo trees that showcase brilliant golden leaves in the fall, Osaka Excel Hotel Tokyu is steps away from popular shopping and leisure activities and a short walk to Hommachi Station. This eco-friendly, Western-style hotel is connected to the centuries-old Namba Betsuin (Minamimido) Temple, beautifully blending modern architecture with treasured historical landmarks – much like Osaka itself. A fitness center, restaurant, and incomparable Japanese hospitality add to its appeal. Highlights of the hotel include: - 364 guestrooms and suites, all centered around color and the concept of kaleidoscopes - A luxurious restaurant on the top floor of the hotel serving seasonal coursed meals - Connected path to the Namba Betsuin Temple, featuring beautiful grounds, events, and more - Opulent bar with pendant lights and a water feature overlooking the Osaka skyline - An on-site gym and special lounge for those staying on the 16th floor with drinks and a library - Central location close to the city’s popular Minami downtown district and Osaka Castle

SPKSE.name: Sapporo Excel Hotel Tokyu
SPKSE.description: A famed winter wonderland, Sapporo, Hokkaido, played host to the 1972 Winter Olympics, leading to worldwide acclaim for the town’s trifecta of assets; winter sports, beer, and Ramen. While the annual Snow Festival is the city’s biggest draw (we pack our parkas and join the millions admiring the ice sculptures), stylish cafes, mouth-watering Ramen, and timeless brews in the Susukino neighborhood make Sapporo far more than just skis and slopes. A quick walk from the city center sits Sapporo Excel Hotel Tokyu, where killer views, large rooms, and easy access to the town's signature sights make this sleek hotspot your best bet to get the most from your Sapporo stay. At a Glance - Walk to nearby Susukino, the heart of Sapporo, to enjoy a nice Genghis Kahn dinner (Mongolian BBQ) - No matter the season, you may find yourself in the midst of beer, food, or snow festivals - Overlook the sparkling city lights from any of 381 guest rooms rising 20 stories into the sky - Fuel up for the day or wind down with a Western or Japanese meal at one of the two on-site restaurants - Summit Mount Moiwa, drink a locally brewed craft beer in Susukino, or visit the historic Clock Tower - Meeting space available holding anywhere from 56 - 160 people for your event

TYOSE.name: Shibuya Excel Hotel Tokyu
TYOSE.address_raw: Shibuya Mark City Building, 1-12-2, Dogenzaka, Tokyo
TYOSE.description: Towering over the famed fashion, commerce, dining, and nightlife of Japan’s lively Shibuya District, Shibuya Excel Hotel Tokyu is truly in the center of all the action. With direct access from the hotel to Shibuya Station via the on-site walkway and the ward’s iconic attractions, including Hachiko Crossing, steps from your room, you can enjoy Tokyo however you choose. Dine sky-high at the on-site restaurants on the 25th floor, experience serenity in the city at the nearby Yoyogi Park, and take in the intoxicating energy of the world’s busiest pedestrian crosswalk during your Japanese journey to Shibuya Excel Hotel Tokyu. At a Glance - Located in the heart of Shibuya, the district is a hub of trendy shops, renowned restaurants, and lively nightlife - Traveling through Tokyo is a breeze with the hotel’s direct pathway to the well-connected Shibuya Station - Take a short stroll to Tokyo’s Times Square, Hachiko Crossing, or enjoy a break from the bustle at nearby Yoyogi Park - Hop a train to Nippon Budokan for a concert or to watch people practice sumo and karate - Three on-site eateries serve everything from French cuisine overlooking the city to authentic Japanese dishes - Function rooms hosts small groups of 10 and larger gatherings of 200+ people for events and conferences

TYOSS.name: SHIBUYA STREAM HOTEL
TYOSS.description: Set in the center of Shibuya, this city getaway is at the cross-section of business, fashion, and entertainment districts. SHIBUYA STREEM HOTEL boasts a beautiful blend of contemporary and classic design with stylish guestrooms and welcoming public areas. The direct connection to Shibuya Train Station puts you in close proximity to all the major hotspots of the city and connects you to all that Tokyo has to offer. - Unwind and refresh in 177 guestrooms beautifully designed to calm yet inspire with attractive décor and attention to detail - Relax in the lobby lounge with friends or colleagues with a drink or bite to eat - Stay in comfort with attentive staff, a health club, and a 24-hour convenience store just minutes away - Explore area attractions such as trendy Shibuya Stream shops and Shibuya Crossing - Hop on the train or drive just 20 minutes to explore the popular areas of Roppongi, Akasaka, and Ginza

FSZST.name: Shimoda Tokyu Hotel
FSZST.description: Overlooking the indigo waters of Oura Bay, this hilltop getaway is surrounded by lush gardens and greenery on the southeast end of Japan’s scenic Izu Peninsula. The island-like area is known for its white-sand beaches and natural hot springs, and your stay at Shimoda Tokyu Hotel places you steps from both of the Peninsula’s iconic features (in fact, an outdoor bath sits just above the beach at the foot of the hill). From panoramic ocean-and-mountain views to the lounger-lined outdoor pool and on-site event space, a serene escape awaits at Shimoda Tokyu Hotel. At a Glance: - Spot the Izu Shichito Islands, neighboring mountains, or surrounding gardens from your room or balcony - Soaring above Oura Bay, walk to the white sand of Shirahama Beach or grab a lounger at the outdoor pool - Relax with an outdoor bath in the natural hot springs overlooking the stunning coastline and beach area - Head to Phoenix for all-day Western dining, hit Hamayu for Japanese cuisine, or enjoy a cocktail at the lounge - Hosting events away from the hustle of the city, wedding halls and conference rooms are available for all occasions

PMISX.name: Son Xotano
PMISX.address_raw: Cami de Ca na Miquela, Mallorca
PMISX.description: Set in Mallorca’s rolling countryside, this Spanish getaway offers an escape from the tourist centers of the island’s coastal regions. Unwind amid greenery and fresh air as you relax by the pool or dine on authentic recipes featuring local, seasonal ingredients. The hotel offers wellness experiences, from massages to yoga and cycling, as well. After a day unwinding in the sun, retreat to your accommodations to enjoy a restful night’s sleep before waking to do it all again. Highlights of the hotel include: - 22 guestrooms and suites, some with a private garden and countryside views - Diverse dining options, including a wine cellar that has been converted into a restaurant - Massage therapists offering in-room or open-air treatments at the hotel - An outdoor swimming pool overlooking the surrounding rural landscape - Activities ranging from biking and hiking local trails to tranquil yoga sessions

DUBSH.name: St Helen’s Hotel
DUBSH.description: Discover moments of serenity all around while at the St Helen’s Hotel. Not far from the city center of Dublin, this hotel is set within 5 acres of expertly landscaped grounds, creating the perfect setting for a peaceful escape from everyday life. The 18th-century residence has been transformed into a luxurious hotel that blends historic elegance with modern amenities for comfortable stays filled with character. From elegant guestrooms and suites filled with natural light – many overlooking the hotel’s lush gardens – to memorable dining experiences, a historic glass-roofed bar, a wellness center with treatments that aid in relaxation, jogging paths, and more, every stay at St Helen’s Hotel feels timeless. Highlights of the hotel include: - 151 guestrooms and suites with high ceilings, soft furnishings, and thoughtful details - A refined Italian restaurant with approachable cuisine, plus seasonal outdoor dining spaces - Home to the White Willow Beauty Clinic, offering rejuvenating treatments, and a fitness center - A Victorian glass-roofed bar serving casual meals and cocktails with an informal ambiance - Sustainable efforts throughout, from reducing waste to using more eco-friendly materials

YXUSP.name: The Park Hotel London
YXUSP.description: With a stylish contemporary design, ample living space, and upscale amenities, The Park Hotel London has everything you need in the heart of downtown. This all-suite hotel offers 126 spacious suites with living and sleeping areas separated by trendy barn doors. In addition to elevated amenities, the hotel offers ample meeting and event spaces. Just a short walk from trendy Richmond Row, a bustling district famous for its cluster of boutique shops, restaurants, and entertainment options, the hotel is perfectly situated for business or leisure. Highlights of the hotel include: - 126 suites, including upgraded options with walk-in glass showers and deluxe spa amenities - Complimentary breakfast plus in-room dining delivered by an American restaurant next door - Well-equipped fitness center offering treadmills, stationary bikes, free weights, and more - A 24-hour lobby market with cold and hot beverages, an array of snacks, and sundry items

HNDTY.name: The Yokohama Bay Hotel Tokyu
HNDTY.description: This bayside getaway in Japan’s famed Minato Mirai 21 perfectly places you steps from Yokohama’s symbolic stops including the neon lights of Cosmo Clock 21 and the shops of Queen’s Square, while making business a breeze with a short stroll to the Pacific Convention Plaza Yokohama and the Minatomirai Station. The Yokohama Bay Hotel Tokyu’s large rooms are complemented by award-winning international cuisine and panoramic city and sea views, an exclusive club lounge, and meeting and event space available for all kinds of occasions. Whether visiting Japan’s second-largest city for work or pleasure, you’ll have the best of both worlds during your seaside stay at The Yokohama Bay Hotel Tokyu. At-A-Glance - Beautiful blue-themed rooms feature views of Memorial Park, the Tokyo Bay’s sparkling waters, or the cityscape - Walk to Cosmo World’s ferris wheel, the shops at Queen’s Square, and the Pacifico Yokohama convention center - Enjoy award-winning Chinese cuisine at Turandot or dishes by Japan top chef Mr. Yutaka Ishinabe at Queen Alice - Swing by the spa to soak in the jacuzzi, swim in the indoor pool, or recover in the sauna - From the lavish Queen's Ballroom to the cozy Pacific Room, meeting space can host groups of 10 to 700+ - Book a Bay Club Floor room and enjoy breakfast, afternoon tea, and drinks from the exclusive Bay Club Lounge

TOYTE.name: Toyama Excel Hotel Tokyu
TOYTE.description: Cherry blossoms, steep mountains, and rugged coastlines form the nature lover’s masterpiece that is the seaside city of Toyama. Packed with glass craftwork (an artistic staple of the area) and interior decor that matches the stunning surroundings, there’s no better place to get to know Japan’s Toyama District than Toyama Excel Hotel Tokyu. From lush landscapes and looming mountain peaks — dinner with a view is the real deal on the hotel’s top floor — to lively shops and eateries, Toyama Excel Hotel Tokyu places you right between the beauty and bustle of the town for a unique escape rivaling your traditional Tokyo trip. At-A-Glance - Head up to the top floor for bird’s eye views of the mountains paired with Japanese and Western cuisines - Hit hotspots like Azuma Castle's garden, the iconic Tenmon-kyo Bridge, and the “King of The Sea,” Kaiwo Maru - Itching to explore? Take advantage of the nearby airport, Horkuriku Shinkansen, and JR Toyama Station - In the heart of Toyama, you’ll be steps from popular restaurants, local shops, and the business district
